title: Diwali (Deepavali) – Festival of Lights
intro: Diwali, also known as Deepavali and the Festival of Lights, is one of the most celebrated Hindu festivals in India and around the world.
description:
Diwali, also known as Deepavali and the Festival of Lights, is one of the most celebrated Hindu festivals in India and around the world. It is observed on the Amavasya (new moon) night of the Kartik month. On this auspicious occasion, people decorate their homes with diyas, rangoli, and lights and worship Goddess Lakshmi and Lord Ganesha to seek happiness, prosperity, and blessings.

According to Hindu traditions, Diwali is celebrated to welcome Lord Rama’s return to Ayodhya after completing 14 years of exile. It is believed that the people of Ayodhya illuminated the entire city with lamps to celebrate his arrival. The festival is also associated with the worship of Goddess Lakshmi, the goddess of wealth and prosperity, and the victory of Lord Krishna over the demon Narakasura.

Diwali represents the victory of light over darkness, good over evil, and knowledge over ignorance. People celebrate this festival by performing prayers, sharing sweets, donating to those in need, and spending joyful moments with family and loved ones.

Festival Month

Kartik Month (October – November)

Significance / Importance

Diwali holds great spiritual and cultural importance in Hinduism. The festival symbolizes the victory of righteousness over negativity and encourages people to welcome positivity and new beginnings in life.

On Diwali, Goddess Lakshmi is worshipped for wealth, prosperity, and abundance, while Lord Ganesha is worshipped for wisdom, success, and the removal of obstacles. Many devotees believe that performing Lakshmi Ganesh Puja on this day brings peace, happiness, and blessings to the home.

For business communities, Diwali is also considered an important occasion for starting new ventures, worshipping account books, and seeking blessings for growth and success. Lighting diyas, decorating homes, exchanging gifts, and helping others are important traditions of this festival.

Puja Vidhi

1. Clean the house and prepare the पूजा area before Diwali Puja.
2. Place idols of Goddess Lakshmi and Lord Ganesha on a clean platform.
3. Offer flowers, fruits, sweets, incense, and diyas to the deities.
4. Perform Lakshmi Ganesh Puja during the शुभ Muhurat.
5. Chant Lakshmi Mantras and Ganesh Mantras with devotion.
6. Light diyas throughout the home to welcome positivity.
7. Perform Lakshmi Aarti and pray for happiness and prosperity.
8. Donate to the needy and share happiness with others.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Lakshmi Mata Aarti
* Ganesh Ji Aarti
* Lakshmi Mantra
* Ganesh Mantra
* Diwali Puja Katha
* Lakshmi Chalisa
* Ganesh Chalisa

faq.a1: Diwali is celebrated on the Amavasya (new moon) day of the Kartik month.
faq.a2: Diwali is celebrated to mark Lord Rama’s return to Ayodhya after 14 years of exile and to worship Goddess Lakshmi.
faq.a3: Goddess Lakshmi and Lord Ganesha are mainly worshipped on Diwali.
faq.q4: In which month is Diwali celebrated?
faq.a4: Diwali is usually celebrated in October or November.
faq.q5: Why is Diwali called the Festival of Lights?
faq.a5: Diwali is called the Festival of Lights because people light diyas and lamps to remove darkness and welcome positivity.
faq.q6: Which mantra should be chanted on Diwali?
faq.a6: Devotees commonly chant Lakshmi Mantra and Ganesh Mantra on Diwali.
faq.q7: What offerings are made during Diwali Puja?
faq.a7: Flowers, fruits, sweets, diyas, and other devotional offerings are offered during Diwali Puja.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Diwali?
faq.a8: Diwali symbolizes the victory of good over evil, light over darkness, and the beginning of a positive new journey.
